data.name: Update to RTO Standards
data.shortDescription: The Department of Employment and Workplace Relations have recently revised the Standards for Registered Training Organisations.
data.content:
The Department of Employment and Workplace Relations have recently revised the Standards for Registered Training Organisations (RTOs). The revised Standards will come into full regulatory effect from **1 July 2025.**

These changes have been designed to increase clarity for RTOs and regulators to promote quality outcomes for candidates and employers.

The revisions to the Standards for RTOs include a change of structure to help provide the VET sector with clear guidance on the requirements to deliver nationally recognised training and comprise of three elements:

- The Outcome Standards
- The Compliance Requirements
- The Credential Policy

Each of the policies above include changes that will affect requirements to RTOs, and trainers and assessors.

Changes that may be relevant for our industries include changes to **The Credential Policy**.

This includes the recognition of new and superseded credentials for the training and assessment of training package products.

These include:

- The recognition of those who hold TAE40110 Certificate IV in Training and Assessment, without the requirement additional units of competency.
- The recognition of the credentials of secondary school teachers to perform certain roles within the VET sector.
- Additional guidance on the use and function of TAE Skill Sets for Volunteer Trainers and/or assessors.

You can find more information on the revised Standards below:

[Revisions to the Standards for Registered Training Organisations - Department of Employment and Workplace Relations, Australian Government](https://www.dewr.gov.au/standards-for-rtos)

To support RTOs with the transition to the revised Standards, the Australian Skills Quality Authority (ASQA) have developed a range of resources that can be found below:

[Preparing for the revised Standards | Australian Skills Quality Authority (ASQA)](https://www.asqa.gov.au/how-we-regulate/revised-standards-rtos/preparing-revised-standards)

data.name: Join Our Strategic Networks
data.shortDescription: Being part of a network provides opportunity for members to collaborate with peers across sectors and regions and to have a direct say on the development of training products and national workforce planning priorities.
data.content:
Public Skills Australia operates across all key phases of growing a skilled workforce. From research, planning and the development of accredited Vocation Education and Training (VET) products, workforce planning, industry stewardship, and other strategic initiatives for Australia’s Government and Public Safety industry-sectors.

[Membership overview](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/38891a89-63b1-4d09-a8bb-3e4b8e65c902.pdf)

Our networks bring diverse subject matter expertise, community perspectives, and lived experience in providing advice on this important work. We are looking to diversify our approach by building the following network groups:

Careers and Pathways network
Diversity and Inclusion network
First Nations network
Registered Training Organisations network
Volunteers network

Being part of a network provides opportunity for members to collaborate with peers across sectors and regions and to have a direct say on the development of training products and national workforce planning priorities. We are seeking nominations by organisations and individuals who are recognised as an authority or advocate in these areas and are interested in supporting our industry-sectors to develop capability. Membership is voluntary with targeted online consultations and focus groups occurring every few months. 

If this opportunity interests you and you have some availability to participate then please get in touch with us using the link below and we will be happy to answer any questions and can send you a nominations form.

[Join Our Strategic Networks](mailto:network@publicskillsaustralia.org.au)
